section.heading: Theft prevention standard for high theft lines

paragraph.chapeau: The Secretary of Transportation by regulation shall prescribe a vehicle theft prevention standard that conforms to the requirements of this chapter. The standard shall apply to—

subparagraph.content: covered major parts that manufacturers install in passenger motor vehicles in lines designated under section 33104 of this title as high theft lines; and
major replacement parts for the major parts described in clause (A) of this paragraph.

paragraph.chapeau: The standard may apply only to—

subparagraph.content: major parts that manufacturers install in passenger motor vehicles having a model year designation later than the calendar year in which the standard takes effect; and
major replacement parts manufactured after the standard takes effect.

subsection.heading: Standard Requirements.—
subsection.content: The standard shall be practicable and provide relevant objective criteria.

subsection.heading: Limitations on Major Part and Replacement Part Standards.—

paragraph.content: For a major part installed by the manufacturer of the motor vehicle, the standard may not require a part to have more than one identification.

paragraph.chapeau: For a major replacement part, the standard may not require—

subparagraph.content: identification of a part not designed as a replacement for a major part required to be identified under the standard; or
the inscribing or affixing of identification except a symbol identifying the manufacturer and a common symbol identifying the part as a major replacement part.

subsection.heading: Records and Reports.—
subsection.content: This chapter does not authorize the Secretary to require a person to keep records or make reports, except as provided in sections 33104(c), 33106(c), 33108(a), and 33112 11 See References in Text note below. of this title.

note.p: In subsection (a)(1), before clause (A), the words “in accordance with this section” are omitted as surplus.
In subsection (a)(2), the text of 15:2022(c)(1)–(3) is omitted as obsolete because the standard has already been prescribed. See 49 C.F.R. part 541.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 33112 of this title, referred to in subsec. (d), was repealed by Pub. L. 112–141, div. C, title I, § 31313(2), July 6, 2012, 126 Stat. 772.
